NAME

server.c - WebSocket server implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures

Initialize WebSocket server. asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run (websocket_server_t *server)
Run WebSocket server event loop. void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t *server)

Detailed Description

WebSocket server implementation.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file lib/network/websocket/server.c.

Function Documentation

void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t * server)

Wake the WebSocket server event loop immediately. Thread-safe. Wakes lws_service() from its timeout wait, allowing the event loop to check the running flag without waiting for the full service timeout (50ms).

Parameters

server Server instance (must have a valid context)

Definition at line 894 of file lib/network/websocket/server.c.

894                                                                  {
895   if (server && server->context) {
896     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
897   }
898 }




References websocket_server::context.


Referenced by server_main().






void
  websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t * server)


Destroy WebSocket server and free resources.


Parameters


server Server instance to destroy


Definition at line 900 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


900                                                           {
901   if (!server) {
902     return;
903   }
904 
905   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, false);
906 
907   // Cancel any pending libwebsockets service calls to interrupt blocking lws_service()
908   // This prevents the event loop from getting stuck in lws_service()
909   if (server->context) {
910     log_debug("[WEBSOCKET_SERVER_DESTROY] Cancelling libwebsockets service");
911     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
912   }
913 
914   // Unregister atomic fields
915   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&server->running);
916 
917   // Destroy handler thread pool (waits for pending work to complete)
918   if (server->handler_pool) {
919     thread_pool_destroy(server->handler_pool);
920     server->handler_pool = NULL;
921   }
922 
923   // Context is normally destroyed by websocket_server_run (from the event loop
924   // thread) for fast shutdown. This handles the case where run() wasn't called
925   // or didn't complete normally.
926   if (server->context) {
927     NAMED_UNREGISTER(server->context);
928     log_debug("WebSocket context still alive in destroy, cleaning up");
929     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
930     server->context = NULL;
931   }
932 
933   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
934 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    websocket_server::context, websocket_server::handler_pool,
    log_debug, NAMED_UNREGISTER, websocket_server::running,
    and thread_pool_destroy().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t websocket_server_init
  (websocket_server_t * server, const websocket_server_config_t *
  config)


Initialize WebSocket server. Creates libwebsockets context and
    prepares for accepting connections.




Parameters


server Server instance to initialize


Config Module Server configuration


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 732 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


732                                                                                                              {
733   if (!server || !config || !config->client_handler) {
734     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
735   }
736 
737   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
738   server->handler = config->client_handler;
739   server->user_data = config->user_data;
740   server->port = config->port;
741   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, true);
742 
743   // Store server pointer in protocol user data so callbacks can access it
744   websocket_protocols[0].user = server; // http protocol
745   websocket_protocols[1].user = server; // acip protocol
746 
747   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
748   lws_log_init_server();
749 
750   // HTTP mount for /health endpoint (required for LWS_CALLBACK_HTTP to fire)
751   static const struct lws_http_mount health_mount = {
752       .mount_next = NULL,
753       .mountpoint = "/",
754       .mountpoint_len = 1,
755       .origin = "http",
756       .def = NULL,
757       .protocol = "http",
758       .origin_protocol = LWSMPRO_CALLBACK,
759   };
760 
761   // Configure libwebsockets context
762   struct lws_context_creation_info info = {0};
763   info.port = config->port;
764   info.protocols = websocket_protocols;
765   info.mounts = &health_mount;
766   info.gid = (gid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
767   info.uid = (uid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
768   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT; // Initialize SSL/TLS support (required for server binding)
769   info.extensions = NULL;                              // Disable permessage-deflate - causing connection issues
770   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &keep_alive_policy; // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
771 
772   // Configure TLS/WSS support if certificates are provided (check for non-empty strings)
773   if (config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0' && config->tls_key_path &&
774       config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0') {
775 #ifdef LWS_WITH_TLS
776     // Validate certificate and key files are readable
777     if (access(config->tls_cert_path, R_OK) != 0) {
778       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS certificate file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_cert_path,
779                        errno);
780     }
781     if (access(config->tls_key_path, R_OK) != 0) {
782       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS key file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_key_path, errno);
783     }
784 
785     info.ssl_cert_filepath = config->tls_cert_path;
786     info.ssl_private_key_filepath = config->tls_key_path;
787     log_info("WebSocket server configured for WSS (TLS): cert=%s, key=%s", config->tls_cert_path, config->tls_key_path);
788 #else
789     log_warn("WebSocket server: TLS support not compiled in libwebsockets; WSS unavailable");
790 #endif
791   } else if ((config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0') ||
792              (config->tls_key_path && config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0')) {
793     log_warn("WebSocket server: Both TLS certificate and key must be provided for WSS; using plain WS");
794   }
795 
796   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
797   // Default is 4KB, causing large frames to fragment into many chunks
798   // Video frames are 921KB, so increase to 2MB to handle them without fragmentation
799   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2MB per-thread service buffer
800 
801   // Disable ALL default timeouts and keep-alive mechanisms
802   // Use only the explicit retry_and_idle_policy we set above (30/35 seconds)
803   info.ka_time = 0;           // Disable TCP keep-alive probes (use WebSocket pings instead)
804   info.ka_probes = 0;         // Disable TCP probes
805   info.ka_interval = 0;       // Disable TCP probe intervals
806   info.keepalive_timeout = 0; // Disable HTTP keep-alive timeout entirely
807 
808   // Explicitly set a very large idle timeout to prevent early disconnection
809   // Without this, libwebsockets defaults to 5 seconds for HTTP connections
810   // Set to effectively infinite (100 hours) since we use retry_and_idle_policy instead
811   info.timeout_secs = 360000; // 100 hours - effectively disabled
812 
813   // Create libwebsockets context
814   // Log diagnostic info before attempting creation
815   log_debug("lws_create_context: port=%u, protocols=%p, options=0x%x", info.port, (void *)info.protocols, info.options);
816 
817   server->context = lws_create_context(&info);
818   if (!server->context) {
819     // Capture any OpenSSL errors that may have occurred during context creation
820     unsigned long ssl_err = 0;
821     char ssl_err_str[256] = "no OpenSSL errors in queue";
822 
823     // Extract all errors from OpenSSL error queue
824     while ((ssl_err = ERR_get_error()) != 0) {
825       ERR_error_string_n(ssl_err, ssl_err_str, sizeof(ssl_err_str));
826       log_error("OpenSSL error: %lu: %s", ssl_err, ssl_err_str);
827     }
828 
829     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to create libwebsockets context (last OpenSSL error: %s)",
830                      ssl_err_str);
831   }
832 
833   // Create thread pool for handling client connections
834   // Use 4 worker threads to handle concurrent client handlers without blocking LWS event loop
835   server->handler_pool = thread_pool_create_with_workers("websocket_handlers", 4);
836   if (!server->handler_pool) {
837     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
838     server->context = NULL;
839     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create WebSocket handler thread pool");
840   }
841 
842   /* Register WebSocket server with named registry */
843   char ws_port_name[32];
844   snprintf(ws_port_name, sizeof(ws_port_name), "ws:%d", server->port);
845   NAMED_REGISTER(server, ws_port_name, "websocket_server", "0x%tx", NULL);
846 
847   /* Register server's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
848   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&server->running, "is_running", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
849 
850   /* Register WebSocket context as child of server */
851   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET(server->context, "context", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
852 
853   log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d with static file serving", config->port);
854   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
855 }

    websocket_server_config_t::client_handler,
    websocket_server::context, errno, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, ERROR_THREAD,
    websocket_server::handler, websocket_server::handler_pool,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    lws_log_init_server(), NAMED_REGISTER,
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET,
    websocket_server::port, websocket_server_config_t::port,
    websocket_server::running, SET_ERRNO,
    thread_pool_create_with_workers(),
    websocket_server_config_t::tls_cert_path,
    websocket_server_config_t::tls_key_path,
    websocket_server::user_data, and
    websocket_server_config_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run
  (websocket_server_t * server)


Run WebSocket server event loop. Blocks until server is signaled
    to stop via running flag.




Parameters


server Server instance


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 857 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


857                                                                    {
858   if (!server || !server->context) {
859     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid server");
860   }
861 
862   log_info("WebSocket server starting event loop on port %d", server->port);
863 
864   // Run libwebsockets event loop
865   uint64_t last_service_ns = 0;
866   int service_call_count = 0;
867   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->running)) {
868     // Service libwebsockets with 16ms timeout to match 60 FPS frame rate.
869     // This provides frequent event processing (~60 callback invocations per second) so that
870     // WebSocket frames queued by the render loop are sent promptly, matching TCP performance.
871     // All client connections share this single server context, so a single lws_service() call
872     // services fragments and events for all clients simultaneously.
873     uint64_t service_start_ns = time_get_ns();
874     if (last_service_ns && service_start_ns - last_service_ns > 30 * US_PER_MS_INT) {
875       // > 30ms gap between service calls
876       double gap_ms = (double)(service_start_ns - last_service_ns) / 1e6;
877       log_info_every(1 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE_GAP] %.1fms gap between lws_service calls", gap_ms);
878     }
879     service_call_count++;
880     log_debug_every(500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE] Call #%d, context=%s", service_call_count,
881                     NAMED_DESCRIBE(server->context, "websocket_server"));
882     int result = lws_service(server->context, 1);
883     if (result < 0) {
884       log_error("libwebsockets service error: %d", result);
885       break;
886     }
887   }
888 
889   log_info(
890       "WebSocket server event loop exited (context will be destroyed by main thread after handler threads complete)");
891   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
892 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_server::context, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    log_debug_every, log_error, log_info,
    log_info_every, NAMED_DESCRIBE, websocket_server::port,
    websocket_server::running, SET_ERRNO, time_get_ns(),
    and US_PER_MS_INT.
